2604.26656.title: Differentially-Private Text Rewriting reshapes Linguistic Style
2604.26656.paper_summary: Differential Privacy (DP) for text matured from disjointed word-level substitutions to contiguous sentence-level rewriting by leveraging the generative capacity of language models. While this form of text privatization is best suited for balancing formal privacy guarantees with grammatical coherence, its impact on the register identity of text remains largely unexplored. By conducting a multidimensional stylistic profiling of differentially-private rewriting, we demonstrate that the cost of privacy extends far beyond lexical variation. Specifically, we find that rewriting under privacy constraints induces a systematic functional mutation of the text's communicative signature. This shift is characterized by the severe attrition of interactive markers, contextual references, and complex subordination. By comparing autoregressive paraphrasing against bidirectional substitution across a spectrum of privacy budgets, we observe that both architectures force convergence toward a non-involved and non-persuasive register. This register-blind sanitization effectively preserves semantic content but structurally homogenizes the nuanced stylistic markers that define human-authored discourse.
2604.26656.paper_summary_zh: 差分隱私（DP）對於文本的發展從不連貫的單詞級替換轉向連續的句子級重寫，利用了語言模型的生成能力。雖然這種文本私密化形式最適合平衡正式的隱私保證與語法連貫性，但其對文本註冊身份的影響仍然大多未被探索。通過對差分私密重寫進行多維度的風格分析，我們證明隱私的成本遠超過詞彙變化。具體來說，我們發現，在隱私限制下的重寫會導致文本傳達特徵的系統性功能突變。這一變化的特徵是互動標記、上下文參考和複雜從屬關係的嚴重減少。通過比較自回歸性改寫與雙向替換在不同隱私預算範圍內的表現，我們觀察到這兩種架構都迫使文本向一種非參與性和非說服性的註冊趨近。這種對註冊盲目的清理有效地保留了語義內容，但在結構上同質化了定義人類創作話語的細微風格標記。
2604.26656.author: Stefan Arnold et.al.
